Produced via `brain/data/life/42shots/velocity/estimate-logic-v3.1.md`
against `baseline-v3.1.md`. Method A only. The spec and durable plan resolve the
lookup-order decisions, so the ×0.2 spec-quality discount and +15% design buffer
apply; implementation values use v3.1's 40% scaling. Calibration is currently
marked stale by `sdlc estimate-source`, so treat this as provisional evidence for
the next recalibration rather than a timeless constant.

## Plan

- [x] Read-set: teach `neighborhood` to return the widened read/completion root
      set (per-artifact ∪ `repo_root`-in-repo-mode ∪ `tool_read_roots`), leaving
      `derive_for_path` (the write/primary root) intact.
- [x] Dispatcher: ordered read-tool lookup consumes the widened read set for
      every supported path shape and read-kind tool;
      write tools keep the narrow root. Update the payload guidance line
      through a shared policy formatter to reflect lookup precedence and the
      read/write split.
- [x] Completion: attach the policy-backed Parley completion source in
      `prep_md` for repo-mode markdown; preserve the existing `prep_chat`
      attachment for global chats; guard against double-attach.
- [x] Tests: read-set includes/excludes repo_root correctly; write-narrow
      rejection holds; `prep_md` attaches; non-repo-mode unchanged.
- [x] Atlas: update `infra/repo_mode.md` #147 section + `providers/tool_use.md`
      cwd-scope note for the read-wide/write-narrow split.

## Log

### 2026-07-11 — implementation

Implemented one ordered root policy consumed by dispatcher enforcement, model
guidance, chat recursion, skill invocation, Vim completion, and a dedicated
nvim-cmp source (`ARCH-DRY`, `ARCH-PURPOSE`). Pure ordering/merging/formatting
stays in `neighborhood`, with filesystem and Neovim work at thin adapters
(`ARCH-PURE`). Verification: `make -f Makefile.parley test-spec
SPEC=providers/tool_use`, `SPEC=skills/skill-system`, `git diff --check`, and
full `make -f Makefile.parley test` passed after the final message-context seam
update.

### 2026-07-10

Created from a brain-side design chat. Decision: **option 1
(read-wide/write-narrow)**, scoped to **all markdown files in repo mode** (not
just repo-backed artifact dirs).

- The self-consistency subtlety that shaped the design: #147 fuses completion +
  tool resolution under one root; a completion-only escape hatch would break that
  (suggest a path the enforcer rejects). Fix is to split the root into a **read
  set** (wide) and a **write root** (narrow) and keep each side self-consistent.
- **Option 2 deferred** (`.parley-neighborhood` marker for an explicit
  intermediate scope between repo-root and file-dir): a different axis — it picks
  a different *single primary* root for a subtree, not a read-set widening.
  Reach for it only if repo-root completion proves too noisy and own-folder too
  narrow for some subtree. YAGNI until a concrete case appears; the two layer
  cleanly and don't conflict.
- Motivating artifact: `brain/data/career/2026/xnurta-plan.md` had
  `Reference: ./` resolving to its own folder with no repo-root path reachable.

## Revisions

### 2026-07-10 — specify ordered multi-root resolution

Reason: fresh-context spec review found that authorizing `repo_root` as an
allowed root would not by itself make a bare repo-relative path resolve there;
the existing dispatcher joins relative inputs only to `cwd`.

Delta: defined neighborhood-first ordered lookup, collision and missing-path
behavior, symlink enforcement, completion ordering/de-duplication, exact path
shapes and dispatcher entry points in scope, and shared model-guidance derivation
(`ARCH-PURPOSE`, `ARCH-PURE`, `ARCH-DRY`).

### 2026-07-10 — reconcile the implementation plan and estimate

Reason: the approved spec is now decomposed into a durable TDD plan, and
`start-plan` requires the estimate to use the current calibrated method before
`change-code`.

Delta: added `workshop/plans/000181-repo-root-read-wide-completion-for-all-markdown-plan.md`
and replaced the provisional v2 estimate with the itemized v3.1 derivation.

### 2026-07-10 — replace cmp-path with one policy-backed cmp source

Reason: plan review verified that nvim-cmp retrieves source configuration by
source name, so multiple `path` entries cannot carry different `get_cwd` roots;
cmp-path also does not enforce the required first-root-wins de-duplication.

Delta: completion still derives entirely from the ordered read roots, but both
Vim and nvim-cmp now consume one shared Parley candidate merger. This preserves
the approved behavior while making the adapter feasible (`ARCH-DRY`,
`ARCH-PURPOSE`).

### 2026-07-11 — repair estimate vocabulary and buffer unit

Reason: `sdlc change-code` correctly rejected two non-canonical primitive slugs;
review also found `design-buffer` had been written as hours rather than the
fraction required by the reconciliation grammar.

Delta: renamed the items to canonical `lua-neovim` / `milestone-review` and set
the planned 15% buffer as `0.15`; the itemized total remains 1.58 hours.

### 2026-07-11 — preserve non-repo markdown and re-estimate visible scope

Reason: the `change-code` plan-quality review found that unconditional
`prep_md` attachment would change ordinary non-repo Markdown, and that the
initial estimate treated the dispatcher/security work and completion adapter as
one small feature despite their separate integration/test surfaces.

Delta: repo-mode `prep_md` attaches completion, global `prep_chat` retains its
# Repo-root Read-wide Completion for All Markdown Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** Consult AGENTS.md Section 3 (Subagent Strategy) to determine the appropriate execution approach: use superpowers-subagent-driven-development (if subagents are suitable per AGENTS.md) or superpowers-executing-plans to implement this plan. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Let reads and file completion search repo-root paths from every repo-mode Markdown buffer while keeping writes confined to the artifact neighborhood.

**Architecture:** Extend `parley.neighborhood` with one ordered `RootPolicy` value containing a narrow `write_root` and widened `read_roots`; every caller consumes that policy rather than reconstructing roots. Keep filesystem probing in the dispatcher/completion adapters, while root derivation, ordering, de-duplication, and model guidance remain directly testable helpers.

**Tech Stack:** Lua, Neovim APIs, Plenary/Busted, nvim-cmp integration.

---

## Core Concepts

### Pure entities

| Name | Lives in | Status |
|------|----------|--------|
| `RootPolicy` | `lua/parley/neighborhood.lua` | new |
| `CompletionCandidates` | `lua/parley/neighborhood.lua` | new |

**RootPolicy** — immutable data `{ write_root, read_roots }` constructed from
already-canonical absolute roots by pure `build_policy(write_root, roots)`.

- **Relationships:** 1:1 with an artifact path at use time; 1:N from one policy to its ordered read roots. The write root is the existing `derive_for_path` result. Read roots are write root, repo root in repo mode, then configured `tool_read_roots`, canonicalized and de-duplicated first-wins.
- **DRY rationale:** `tool_loop`, `skill_invoke`, completion, and model context currently receive pieces of path policy separately. A shared value prevents enforcement and guidance from reconstructing different root sets (`ARCH-DRY`). Filesystem-dependent canonicalization stays in `policy_for_path`; the pure builder only orders and de-duplicates supplied strings (`ARCH-PURE`).
- **Future extensions:** A subtree marker can change policy construction later without changing dispatcher or completion consumers.

**CompletionCandidates** — ordered root-relative string labels merged from
per-root filesystem results; adapters wrap strings as cmp items only at the UI
boundary.

- **Relationships:** N:1 from per-root match lists to one de-duplicated display list; the earliest root owns a collision.
- **DRY rationale:** Both built-in completion and cmp configuration need the same ordering and collision rule.
- **Future extensions:** Candidate metadata can later expose the owning root or ranking without changing root derivation.

### Integration points

| Name | Lives in | Status | Wraps |
|------|----------|--------|-------|
| `ReadPathResolver` | `lua/parley/tools/dispatcher.lua` | modified | filesystem existence, realpath, and containment checks |
| `ArtifactRootPolicy` | `lua/parley/neighborhood.lua` | modified | path normalization, buffer name, live config, and chat-root discovery |
| `MarkdownCompletionAttach` | `lua/parley/neighborhood.lua`, `lua/parley/init.lua` | modified | Neovim completefunc/autocmds and nvim-cmp |
| `ToolPolicyWiring` | `lua/parley/tool_loop.lua`, `lua/parley/chat_respond.lua`, `lua/parley/skill_invoke.lua` | modified | chat and skill tool dispatch plus model payload |

**ReadPathResolver** — resolves relative reads by probing each ordered root and accepting the first existing, contained real path; absolute reads must lie within one root. Writes continue through `resolve_path_in_cwd`, including its missing-leaf behavior.

- **Injected into:** `dispatcher.execute_call` through `opts.root_policy`; tests use temporary directories and real symlinks rather than mocking filesystem calls.
- **Future extensions:** Additional read path shapes still enter through the dispatcher's single prelude.

**ArtifactRootPolicy** — thin adapter that canonicalizes configured roots and calls the pure policy builder; `policy_for_buf` adds live buffer/config discovery.

- **Injected into:** tool dispatch, completion attachment, and payload construction.
- **Future extensions:** Buffer-local policy invalidation if repo configuration becomes mutable while a buffer is open.

**MarkdownCompletionAttach** — stores the policy buffer-locally, installs one completefunc/InsertEnter hook, and registers one Parley cmp source backed by the shared candidate merger.

- **Injected into:** repo-mode `prep_md`; `prep_chat` retains the existing call
  for global chats and becomes an idempotent no-op after repo-mode `prep_md`.
- **Future extensions:** Candidate metadata and ranking can widen inside the dedicated source without changing attachment.

**ToolPolicyWiring** — passes one policy to the dispatcher and formats model context from that same value.

- **Injected into:** `tool_loop.process_response`, `skill_invoke.invoke`, `_build_messages`, and `build_messages_from_model`.
- **Future extensions:** Structured provider metadata can replace prose without changing policy semantics.

## Chunk 1: Root Policy and Read Resolution

### Task 1: Derive the ordered root policy

**Files:**
- Modify: `lua/parley/neighborhood.lua`
- Test: `tests/unit/neighborhood_spec.lua`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing pure policy-builder tests**

Test only `build_policy(write_root, roots)`: write root is retained, root order is
stable, duplicate/blank roots are removed first-wins, and inputs are not mutated.

- [ ] **Step 2: Write failing filesystem-adapter tests**

Add `policy_for_path(path, config, chat_roots)` cases proving:

```lua
assert.same({
    write_root = "/repo/data/career",
    read_roots = { "/repo/data/career", "/repo", "/repo-sibling" },
}, neighborhood.policy_for_path(
    "/repo/data/career/note.md",
    { repo_root = "/repo", tool_read_roots = { "/repo", "../../../repo-sibling" } },
    {}
))
```

Also cover repo-backed artifacts (write root and repo root collapse to one),
non-repo mode, invalid paths, and absolute/`~`/relative configured roots.

- [ ] **Step 3: Run the focused spec and verify RED**

Run: `make test-spec SPEC=neighborhood`

Expected: non-zero exit with `attempt to call field 'build_policy' (a nil value)`
from the named policy test.

- [ ] **Step 4: Implement the pure policy builder**

Keep `derive_for_path` unchanged as the narrow source. Add a pure constructor:

```lua
function M.build_policy(write_root, ordered_roots)
    local seen, read_roots = {}, {}
    for _, root in ipairs(ordered_roots or {}) do
        if type(root) == "string" and root ~= "" and not seen[root] then
            seen[root] = true
            read_roots[#read_roots + 1] = root
        end
    end
    return { write_root = write_root, read_roots = read_roots }
end
```

Expected implementation is the `build_policy` function above only.

- [ ] **Step 5: Implement shared canonical policy construction**

Add `canonical_roots(write_root, repo_root, configured_roots) -> string[]`.
It emits candidates strictly as `write_root`, then `repo_root` when nonblank,
then configured roots in list order. Absolute roots normalize directly; `~/x`
expands before normalization; relative roots join to `write_root`. Each uses
`fs_realpath` when it exists and otherwise the normalized absolute path. The
helper returns canonical candidates without de-duplication; `build_policy`
performs the one pure first-wins fold.

Export `policy_from_roots(write_root, repo_root, configured_roots) -> RootPolicy`
as the only public canonicalization entry point; it calls private
`canonical_roots` and then `build_policy`. Both `policy_for_path` and the
temporary dispatcher adapter must call this API, so dispatcher never reaches a
private helper or duplicates root normalization.

- [ ] **Step 6: Implement the artifact adapter**

Add:

```lua
function M.policy_for_path(path, config, chat_roots)
    local write_root, err = M.derive_for_path(path, config, chat_roots)
    if not write_root then return nil, err end
    return M.policy_from_roots(write_root, config and config.repo_root,
        config and config.tool_read_roots)
end
```

Add `policy_for_buf(buf)` in a separate edit as the thin live-state wrapper and
keep `for_buf(buf)` returning only `write_root` until Chunk 2 migration.

- [ ] **Step 7: Run focused tests and verify GREEN**

Run: `make test-spec SPEC=neighborhood`

Expected: exit 0 with zero failed assertions in `tests/unit/neighborhood_spec.lua`.

- [ ] **Step 8: Commit the policy core**

```bash
git add lua/parley/neighborhood.lua tests/unit/neighborhood_spec.lua
git commit -m "#181: derive ordered neighborhood root policy"
```

### Task 2: Resolve read paths across ordered roots

**Files:**
- Modify: `lua/parley/tools/dispatcher.lua`
- Test: `tests/unit/tools_dispatcher_spec.lua`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing relative lookup and collision tests**

Add `resolve_read_path(path, read_roots)` tests for:

- a bare `atlas/index.md` absent under neighborhood but present under repo root;
- the same relative path under both roots (neighborhood wins);

- [ ] **Step 2: Write failing missing/absolute/symlink tests**

Add separate cases for missing relative reads, absolute reads inside/outside the
set, a symlink escaping every root, and a symlink resolving into another allowed
root.

- [ ] **Step 3: Run dispatcher tests and verify RED**

Run: `make test-spec SPEC=providers/tool_use`

Expected: non-zero exit with `attempt to call field 'resolve_read_path' (a nil value)`
from the new resolver test.

- [ ] **Step 4: Implement the read-only resolver**

Add `resolve_read_path(path, read_roots) -> abs_path|nil, err` beside
`resolve_path_in_cwd`. For relative input, loop roots in order, join and
`fs_realpath` each candidate, and return the first existing candidate whose real
path is contained by any canonical root. For absolute input, require
`fs_realpath(path)` and validate it against the full set. If no relative
candidate exists, return `nil, "read path not found in configured roots: " ..
path`. Preserve `resolve_path_in_cwd` unchanged for writes/new files.

- [ ] **Step 5: Add and test the legacy option adapter**

`policy_from_opts(opts)` must transform legacy `{ cwd, read_roots }` via
`neighborhood.policy_from_roots(cwd, nil, read_roots)`: cwd first,
each relative read root resolved against cwd, canonical first-wins order. Test
`cwd=/repo/data` plus `read_roots={"../", "/repo"}` yields one `/repo/data`
then one `/repo` and resolves `atlas/index.md` from `/repo`.

- [ ] **Step 6: Migrate scalar dispatcher fields**

Change `execute_call` to accept `opts.root_policy`. Route read `path` and
`file_path` through `resolve_read_path`; route write scalars through
`resolve_path_in_cwd(policy.write_root)`.

- [ ] **Step 7: Migrate arrays and defaults**

    88	                return owner
    89	            end
    90	        end
    91	    end
    92	
    93	    return dirname(artifact_path)
    94	end
    95	
    96	function M.build_policy(write_root, ordered_roots)
    97	    local seen, read_roots = {}, {}
    98	    for _, root in ipairs(ordered_roots or {}) do
    99	        if type(root) == "string" and root ~= "" and not seen[root] then
   100	            seen[root] = true
   101	            read_roots[#read_roots + 1] = root
   102	        end
   103	    end
   104	    return { write_root = write_root, read_roots = read_roots }
   105	end
   106	
   107	function M.policy_from_roots(write_root, repo_root, configured_roots)
   108	    local function canonical(path)
   109	        path = clean(path)
   110	        return path and (vim.loop.fs_realpath(path) or path) or nil
   111	    end
   112	    write_root = canonical(write_root)
   113	    if not write_root then return nil, "buffer has no file" end
   114	    local roots = { write_root }
   115	    repo_root = canonical(repo_root)
   116	    if repo_root then roots[#roots + 1] = repo_root end
   117	    for _, root in ipairs(configured_roots or {}) do
   118	        if type(root) == "string" and root ~= "" then
   119	            if root:sub(1, 1) == "~" then root = vim.fn.expand(root) end
   120	            local resolved = root:sub(1, 1) == "/" and root or join(write_root, root)
   121	            roots[#roots + 1] = canonical(resolved)
   122	        end
   123	    end
   124	    return M.build_policy(write_root, roots)
   125	end
   126	
   127	function M.policy_for_path(path, config, chat_roots)
   128	    local write_root, err = M.derive_for_path(path, config, chat_roots)
   129	    if not write_root then return nil, err end
   130	    local configured_repo = clean(config and config.repo_root)
   131	    local repo_root = configured_repo and path_within(path, configured_repo)
   132	        and configured_repo or nil
   133	    return M.policy_from_roots(write_root, repo_root,
   134	        config and config.tool_read_roots)
   135	end
   136	
   137	function M.format_tool_context(policy)
   138	    if not policy or not policy.write_root then return nil end
   139	    local lines = { "Relative reads search these roots in order (first existing match wins):" }
   140	    for _, root in ipairs(policy.read_roots or {}) do lines[#lines + 1] = "- " .. root end
   141	    lines[#lines + 1] = "Relative writes resolve only from: " .. policy.write_root
   142	    return table.concat(lines, "\n")
   143	end
   144	
   145	function M.for_buf(buf)
   146	    local path = vim.api.nvim_buf_get_name(buf)
   147	    local config = require("parley").config
   148	    local ok, chat_dirs = pcall(require, "parley.chat_dirs")
   149	    local roots = {}
   150	    if ok and type(chat_dirs.get_chat_roots) == "function" then
   151	        local roots_ok, derived_roots = pcall(chat_dirs.get_chat_roots)
   152	        if roots_ok then
   153	            roots = derived_roots
   154	        end
   155	    end
   156	    return M.derive_for_path(path, config, roots)
   157	end
   158	
   159	function M.policy_for_buf(buf)
   160	    local path = vim.api.nvim_buf_get_name(buf)
   161	    local config = require("parley").config
   162	    local ok, chat_dirs = pcall(require, "parley.chat_dirs")
   163	    local roots = {}
   164	    if ok and type(chat_dirs.get_chat_roots) == "function" then
   165	        local roots_ok, derived_roots = pcall(chat_dirs.get_chat_roots)
   166	        if roots_ok then roots = derived_roots end
   167	    end
   168	    return M.policy_for_path(path, config, roots)
   169	end
   170	
   171	local function relative_to_root(path, root)
   172	    path = clean(path)
   173	    root = clean(root)
   174	    if not path or not root then
   175	        return nil
   176	    end
   177	    if path == root then
   178	        return ""
   179	    end
   180	    if path:sub(1, #root + 1) == root .. "/" then
   181	        return path:sub(#root + 2)
   182	    end
   183	    return nil
   184	end
   185	
   186	local function policy_for_completion(buf)
   187	    return vim.b[buf].parley_root_policy or M.policy_for_buf(buf)
   188	end
   189	
   190	function M.merge_completion_candidates(per_root)
   191	    local seen, out = {}, {}
   192	    for _, items in ipairs(per_root or {}) do
   193	        local sorted = vim.deepcopy(items)
   194	        table.sort(sorted)
   195	        for _, item in ipairs(sorted) do
   196	            if not seen[item] then seen[item] = true; out[#out + 1] = item end
   197	        end
   198	    end
   199	    return out
   200	end
   201	
   202	function M.completion_candidates(policy, base)
   203	    local groups = {}
   204	    for _, root in ipairs(policy and policy.read_roots or {}) do
   205	        local items = {}
   206	        for _, match in ipairs(vim.fn.glob(root .. "/" .. (base or "") .. "*", false, true)) do
   207	            local rel = relative_to_root(match, root)
   208	            if rel and rel ~= "" then
   209	                if vim.fn.isdirectory(match) == 1 then rel = rel .. "/" end
   210	                items[#items + 1] = rel
   211	            end
   212	        end
   213	        groups[#groups + 1] = items
   214	    end
   215	    local accepted = {}
   216	    local resolver = require("parley.tools.dispatcher").resolve_read_path
   217	    for _, label in ipairs(M.merge_completion_candidates(groups)) do
   218	        if resolver(label:gsub("/$", ""), policy.read_roots) then
   219	            accepted[#accepted + 1] = label
   220	        end
   221	    end
   222	    return accepted
   223	end
   224	
   225	function M.completefunc(findstart, base)
   226	    if tonumber(findstart) == 1 then
   227	        local line = vim.api.nvim_get_current_line()
   228	        local col = vim.fn.col(".") - 1
   229	        local start = col
   230	        while start > 0 do
   231	            local ch = line:sub(start, start)
   232	            if ch:match("[%s%(%[%{]") then
   233	                break
   234	            end
   235	            start = start - 1
   236	        end
   237	        return start
   238	    end
   239	
   240	    local buf = vim.api.nvim_get_current_buf()
   241	    local policy = policy_for_completion(buf)
   242	    if not policy then
   243	        return {}
   244	    end
   245	    return M.completion_candidates(policy, base)
   246	end
   247	
   248	local cmp_registered = false
   249	local function cmp_path_sources(cmp)
   250	    local sources = {
   251	        {
   252	            name = "parley_path",
   253	        },
   254	        { name = "buffer" },
   255	    }
   256	
   257	    if cmp.config and type(cmp.config.sources) == "function" then
   258	        return cmp.config.sources(sources)
   259	    end
   260	    return sources
   261	end
   262	
   263	function M.attach_cmp_completion(buf)
   264	    if not vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(buf) then
   265	        return nil
   266	    end
   267	
   268	    local policy = policy_for_completion(buf)
   269	    if not policy then
   270	        return nil
   271	    end
   272	
   273	    local ok, cmp = pcall(require, "cmp")
   274	    if not ok or type(cmp) ~= "table" or type(cmp.setup) ~= "table" or type(cmp.setup.buffer) ~= "function" then
   275	        return nil
   276	    end
   277	    if not cmp_registered and type(cmp.register_source) == "function" then
   278	        cmp.register_source("parley_path", {
   279	            complete = function(_, params, callback)
   280	                local target = params.context and params.context.bufnr or vim.api.nvim_get_current_buf()
   281	                local before = params.context and params.context.cursor_before_line or ""
   282	                local base = before:match("([^%s%(%[%{]+)$") or ""
   283	                local words = M.completion_candidates(policy_for_completion(target), base)
   284	                local items = {}
   285	                for _, word in ipairs(words) do items[#items + 1] = { label = word, word = word } end
   286	                callback(items)
   287	            end,
   288	        })
   289	        cmp_registered = true
   290	    end
   291	
   292	    cmp.setup.buffer({
   293	        completion = {
   294	            keyword_pattern = [[\~\?\(\k\|[\/\.\-]\)\+]],
   295	            keyword_length = 1,
   296	        },
   297	        sources = cmp_path_sources(cmp),
   298	    })
   299	    return policy.write_root
   300	end
   301	
   302	local function schedule_cmp_attach(buf)
   303	    vim.schedule(function()
   304	        M.attach_cmp_completion(buf)
   305	    end)
   306	end
   307	
   308	function M.attach_completion(buf)
   309	    local policy = M.policy_for_buf(buf)
   310	    if not policy then
   311	        return nil
   312	    end
   313	    if vim.b[buf].parley_completion_attached then return policy.write_root end
   314	    vim.b[buf].parley_completion_attached = true
   315	    vim.b[buf].parley_root_policy = policy
   316	    vim.api.nvim_set_option_value("completefunc", "v:lua.require'parley.neighborhood'.completefunc", { buf = buf })
   317	    schedule_cmp_attach(buf)
   318	    vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd("InsertEnter", {
   319	        buffer = buf,
   320	        once = true,
   321	        callback = function()
   322	            schedule_cmp_attach(buf)
   323	        end,
   324	    })
   325	    return policy.write_root
   326	end
   327	
   328	return M
     1	-- Tool dispatcher — the DRY safety layer between tool_loop and
     2	-- individual handler functions.
     3	--
     4	-- Handlers (lua/parley/tools/builtin/*.lua) are pure. They know
     5	-- nothing about cwd-scope, symlink resolution, truncation, or
     6	-- error wrapping. Every safety concern lives HERE so there's
     7	-- exactly one place to audit and one place to fix.
     8	--
     9	-- At M2 this module exposes read-path helpers (resolve_path_in_cwd,
    10	-- truncate, execute_call). Write-path concerns (dirty-buffer guard,
    11	-- .parley-backup, gitignore auto-append, checktime-reload,
    12	-- metadata-preserving truncation for write_file's pre-image footer)
    13	-- land in M5 Task 5.1 / 5.2 / 5.3 / 5.6 and extend this same file.
    14	--
    15	-- SINGLE source for each invariant:
    16	--   - cwd-scope + symlink safety: resolve_path_in_cwd
    17	--   - result size cap:            truncate / truncate_preserving_footer (M5)
    18	--   - pcall-guarded handler call: execute_call
    19	--   - dirty-buffer guard:         check_dirty_buffer (M5)
    20	--   - pre-image capture:          ensure_backup (M5)
    21	--   - post-write reload:          _checktime_if_loaded (M5)
    22	
    23	local M = {}
    24	
    25	local types = require("parley.tools.types")
    26	
    27	--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    28	-- Path resolution
    29	--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    30	
    31	-- Resolve a configured read root to a canonical absolute path. Roots may be
    32	-- absolute (`/x`), home-relative (`~/x`, `~` expanded), or relative to cwd
    33	-- (`../`, `sub/dir`). Returns the realpath, the normalized path if it does not
    34	-- resolve, or nil for an invalid root. (#140)
    35	local function resolve_root(root, cwd)
    36	    if type(root) ~= "string" or root == "" then
    37	        return nil
    38	    end
    39	    if root:sub(1, 1) == "~" then
    40	        root = vim.fn.expand(root)
    41	    end
    42	    local abs = root:sub(1, 1) == "/" and vim.fs.normalize(root)
    43	        or vim.fs.normalize(cwd .. "/" .. root)
    44	    return vim.loop.fs_realpath(abs) or abs
    45	end
    46	
    47	--- Resolve a possibly-relative path against cwd, normalize, resolve
    48	--- symlinks via fs_realpath, and reject anything whose real path
    49	--- escapes cwd (and every configured read root).
    50	---
    51	--- Handles the three cases the tool loop needs:
    52	---
    53	---   1. Existing file inside cwd → returns canonical realpath
    54	---   2. Existing file outside cwd (or symlink resolving outside) → rejected
    55	---   3. NEW file (doesn't exist yet) inside cwd → returns
    56	---      `realpath(parent) .. "/" .. basename`. This is what
    57	---      write_file needs when creating a fresh file: the file
    58	---      itself doesn't exist, but its parent dir does, and that
    59	---      parent must be inside cwd.
    60	---
    61	--- `allowed_roots` (read tools only, #140): extra roots a path may resolve
    62	--- under, in addition to cwd. Each is resolved + symlink-canonicalized, so a
    63	--- symlink escaping every root is still rejected. nil/empty → cwd-only.
    64	---
    65	--- Returns `abs_path` on success, or `(nil, err_msg)` on rejection.
    66	---
    67	--- @param path string
    68	--- @param cwd string
    69	--- @param allowed_roots string[]|nil  extra read roots (absolute/~/relative-to-cwd)
    70	--- @return string|nil abs_path
    71	--- @return string|nil err_msg
    72	function M.resolve_path_in_cwd(path, cwd, allowed_roots)
    73	    if type(path) ~= "string" or path == "" then
    74	        return nil, "path must be a non-empty string"
    75	    end
    76	
    77	    -- Normalize the joined path lexically first, so "../" and "./"
    78	    -- segments are collapsed before we hit the filesystem.
    79	    local joined
    80	    if path:sub(1, 1) == "/" then
    81	        joined = vim.fs.normalize(path)
    82	    else
    83	        joined = vim.fs.normalize(cwd .. "/" .. path)
    84	    end
    85	
    86	    -- Resolve symlinks for the PATH we care about. If the path
    87	    -- doesn't exist (new file creation), fall back to realpath-ing
    88	    -- the parent directory and appending the basename.
    89	    local real_path = vim.loop.fs_realpath(joined)
    90	    if not real_path then
    91	        local parent = vim.fs.dirname(joined)
    92	        local real_parent = vim.loop.fs_realpath(parent)
    93	        if not real_parent then
    94	            return nil, "cannot resolve parent directory: " .. parent
    95	        end
    96	        real_path = real_parent .. "/" .. vim.fs.basename(joined)
    97	    end
    98	
    99	    -- Resolve the cwd too so the comparison is between two canonical
   100	    -- absolute paths (handles symlinked /tmp → /private/tmp on macOS).
   101	    local real_cwd = vim.loop.fs_realpath(cwd) or vim.fs.normalize(cwd)
   102	
   103	    -- Allowed base dirs: cwd, plus any configured read roots (#140). A path is
   104	    -- inside a base iff it equals the base OR starts with base + "/". String
   105	    -- comparison is safe because both sides are canonical fs_realpath outputs.
   106	    local bases = { real_cwd }
   107	    for _, root in ipairs(allowed_roots or {}) do
   108	        local r = resolve_root(root, cwd)
   109	        if r then
   110	            table.insert(bases, r)
   111	        end
   112	    end
   113	
   114	    for _, base in ipairs(bases) do
   115	        if real_path == base or real_path:sub(1, #base + 1) == base .. "/" then
   116	            return real_path
   117	        end
   118	    end
   119	
   120	    if allowed_roots then
   121	        return nil, "path outside working directory and configured read roots: "
   122	            .. path .. " (add a root to parley `tool_read_roots` to allow it)"
   123	    end
   124	    return nil, "path outside working directory: " .. path
   125	end
   126	
   127	function M.resolve_read_path(path, read_roots)
   128	    if type(path) ~= "string" or path == "" then
   129	        return nil, "path must be a non-empty string"
   130	    end
   131	    local roots = {}
   132	    for _, root in ipairs(read_roots or {}) do
   133	        local resolved = resolve_root(root, root)
   134	        if resolved then roots[#roots + 1] = resolved end
   135	    end
   136	    local candidates = {}
   137	    if path:sub(1, 1) == "/" then
   138	        candidates[1] = path
   139	    else
   140	        for _, root in ipairs(roots) do candidates[#candidates + 1] = root .. "/" .. path end
   141	    end
   142	    for _, candidate in ipairs(candidates) do
   143	        candidate = vim.fs.normalize(candidate)
   144	        if vim.loop.fs_lstat(candidate) then
   145	            local real = vim.loop.fs_realpath(candidate)
   146	            if not real then
   147	                return nil, "cannot resolve read path: " .. path
   148	            end
   149	            for _, root in ipairs(roots) do
   150	                if real == root or real:sub(1, #root + 1) == root .. "/" then return real end
   151	            end
   152	            return nil, "read path resolves outside configured roots: " .. path
   153	        end
   154	    end
   155	    return nil, "read path not found in configured roots: " .. path
   156	end
   157	
   158	--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   159	-- Result truncation
   160	--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   161	
   162	--- Byte-length truncation with a trailing marker.
   163	---
   164	--- Used by execute_call to cap the size of each ToolResult at
   165	--- `opts.max_bytes` (default 100KB via the agent config). M5 will
   166	--- add a metadata-preserving variant (truncate_preserving_footer)
   167	--- that write_file uses to keep its `pre-image:` footer intact.
   168	---
   169	--- Pure. Handles nil content as empty string.
   170	---
   171	--- @param content string|nil
   172	--- @param max_bytes number
   173	--- @return string
   174	function M.truncate(content, max_bytes)
   175	    content = content or ""
   176	    if #content <= max_bytes then return content end
   177	    local omitted = #content - max_bytes
   178	    return content:sub(1, max_bytes) .. string.format("\n... [truncated: %d bytes omitted]", omitted)
   179	end
   180	
   181	-- #139: horizontal output pager. Window `content` to lines [offset, offset+limit)
   182	-- (offset 1-indexed) and, when the window doesn't cover the whole output, append a
   183	-- footer naming the true total + how to page/narrow. Pure. Returns the windowed
   184	-- string (with footer) and the total line count.
   185	M.PAGE_DEFAULT_LIMIT = 200
   186	M.PAGE_MAX_LIMIT = 2000
   187	
   188	function M.page_lines(content, offset, limit)
   189	    content = content or ""
   190	    local lines = vim.split(content, "\n", { plain = true })
   191	    -- A trailing newline yields a spurious empty final element; drop it so the
   192	    -- count matches the visible lines.
   193	    if #lines > 1 and lines[#lines] == "" then
   194	        table.remove(lines)
   195	    end
   196	    local total = #lines
   197	    offset = math.max(1, math.floor(offset or 1))
   198	    limit = math.max(1, math.floor(limit or M.PAGE_DEFAULT_LIMIT))
   199	
   200	    if offset > total then
   201	        return "... [no lines at offset " .. offset .. "; output has " .. total .. " line(s)]", total
   202	    end
   203	
   204	    local last = math.min(offset + limit - 1, total)
   205	    local window = {}
   206	    for i = offset, last do
   207	        window[#window + 1] = lines[i]
   208	    end
   209	    local text = table.concat(window, "\n")
   210	
   211	    local windowed = (offset > 1) or (last < total)
   212	    if windowed then
   213	        local note = (last < total)
   214	            and (" — pass offset=" .. (last + 1) .. " for the next page, or narrow your query")
   215	            or " — end of output"
   216	        text = text .. "\n... [lines " .. offset .. "-" .. last .. " of " .. total .. note .. "]"
   217	    end
   218	    return text, total
   219	end
   220	
   221	--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   222	-- Handler invocation
   223	--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   224	
   225	--- Execute a ToolCall against the registered handler, with:
   226	---   - registry lookup (is_error on unknown name)
   227	---   - pcall around handler (is_error on raise)
   228	---   - non-table return guard (is_error on misbehaving handler)
   229	---   - id/name stamping on the returned result
   230	---   - byte-length truncation when opts.max_bytes is set
   231	---
   232	--- The returned ToolResult is ALWAYS well-shaped even when things
   233	--- go wrong — the tool loop driver can serialize it directly without
   234	--- further checks.
   235	---
   236	--- M5 will add a write-path prelude to this function (cwd-scope,
   237	--- dirty-buffer, backup, gitignore, checktime) branched on
   238	--- `def.kind == "write"`. At M2 the shared prelude only handles
   239	--- the cwd-scope check when `call.input.path` is present.
   240	---
   241	--- @param call ToolCall { id, name, input }
   242	--- @param tools_registry table module exposing `get(name)` (parley.tools)
   243	--- @param opts table|nil { max_bytes?: number, cwd?: string }
   244	--- @return ToolResult
   245	function M.execute_call(call, tools_registry, opts)
   246	    opts = opts or {}
   247	    local policy = opts.root_policy
   248	    if not policy and opts.cwd then
   249	        policy = require("parley.neighborhood").policy_from_roots(opts.cwd, nil, opts.read_roots)
   250	    end
   251	
   252	    local def = tools_registry.get(call.name)
   253	    if not def then
   254	        return {
   255	            id = call.id,
   256	            name = call.name,
   257	            content = "Tool '" .. call.name .. "' is not available on this client. Please continue without it.",
   258	            is_error = true,
   259	        }
   260	    end
   261	
   262	    -- SHARED PRELUDE: cwd-scope check for any tool whose input has a
   263	    -- `path` string field. Read tools additionally honor configured
   264	    -- `tool_read_roots` (#140); write tools stay cwd-confined.
   265	    -- (M5 adds write-specific additional guards on top of this.)
   266	    --
   267	    -- `opts.cwd` is optional — the tool_loop passes it explicitly so
   268	    -- the dispatcher does not need to know about vim.fn.getcwd() from
   269	    -- pure test contexts. When absent, the check is skipped (caller
   270	    -- accepts responsibility).
   271	    -- Resolve path fields: tools may use `path` or `file_path`.
   272	    -- Check both so the cwd-scope guard applies uniformly.
   273	    local function roots_for_def()
   274	        -- #140: read tools may also reach any configured `tool_read_roots`;
   275	        -- write tools get nil → cwd-only. Gate on `~= "write"` (the canonical
   276	        -- read-tool predicate `@readonly` uses): `kind` defaults to read when
   277	        -- absent, so `== "read"` would wrongly confine an absent-kind tool.
   278	        return (def.kind ~= "write") and (policy and policy.read_roots or {}) or nil
   279	    end
   280	
   281	    local path_fields = { "path", "file_path" }
   282	    if policy and call.input and def.default_path and call.input.path == nil
   283	        and call.input.file_path == nil and call.input.paths == nil then
   284	        call.input.path = def.default_path
   285	    end
   286	    for _, field in ipairs(path_fields) do
   287	        if policy and call.input and type(call.input[field]) == "string" then
   288	            local roots = roots_for_def()
   289	            local abs, scope_err
   290	            if def.kind ~= "write" then
   291	                abs, scope_err = M.resolve_read_path(call.input[field], roots)
   292	            else
   293	                abs, scope_err = M.resolve_path_in_cwd(call.input[field], policy.write_root)
   294	            end
   295	            if not abs then
   296	                return {
   297	                    id = call.id,
   298	                    name = call.name,
   299	                    content = scope_err,
   300	                    is_error = true,
   301	                }
   302	            end
   303	            call.input[field] = abs
   304	        end
   305	    end
   306	    if policy and call.input and type(call.input.paths) == "table" then
   307	        local roots = roots_for_def()
   308	        local resolved = {}
   309	        for i, path in ipairs(call.input.paths) do
   310	            if type(path) ~= "string" then
   311	                return {
   312	                    id = call.id,
   313	                    name = call.name,
   314	                    content = "paths must be an array of strings",
   315	                    is_error = true,
   316	                }
   317	            end
   318	            local abs, scope_err
   319	            if def.kind ~= "write" then
   320	                abs, scope_err = M.resolve_read_path(path, roots)
   321	            else
   322	                abs, scope_err = M.resolve_path_in_cwd(path, policy.write_root)
   323	            end
   324	            if not abs then
   325	                return {
   326	                    id = call.id,
   327	                    name = call.name,
   328	                    content = scope_err,
   329	                    is_error = true,
   330	                }
   331	            end
   332	            resolved[i] = abs
   333	        end
   334	        call.input.paths = resolved
   335	    end
   336	
   337	    -- #139: horizontal output pager. For tools that don't self-paginate, take
   338	    -- offset/limit out of the input (the handler never sees them) and apply them
   339	    -- to the handler's OUTPUT below. read_file self_paginates → it pages natively.
   340	    local page = nil
   341	    if types.is_pageable(def) then -- #139: non-write, non-self-paginating only
   342	        local default_limit = opts.page_limit or M.PAGE_DEFAULT_LIMIT
   343	        local in_limit = tonumber((call.input or {}).limit) or default_limit
   344	        page = {
   345	            offset = tonumber((call.input or {}).offset) or 1,
   346	            limit = math.min(in_limit, M.PAGE_MAX_LIMIT),
   347	        }
   348	        if call.input then
   349	            call.input.offset = nil
   350	            call.input.limit = nil
   351	        end
   352	    end
   353	
   354	    -- HANDLER invocation, pcall-guarded. A raising handler becomes an
   355	    -- error ToolResult rather than propagating the error up to the
   356	    -- tool loop (which would leave an orphan 🔧: block and break the
   357	    -- cancel-cleanup invariant).
   358	    local ok, result = pcall(def.handler, call.input or {})
   359	    if not ok then
   360	        return {
  1420	
  1421	-- Export current chat buffer as HTML (delegated to exporter)
  1422	M.cmd.ExportHTML = function(params)
  1423		exporter.export_html(params)
  1424	end
  1425	
  1426	
  1427	-- Export current chat buffer as Markdown for Jekyll (delegated to exporter)
  1428	M.cmd.ExportMarkdown = function(params)
  1429		exporter.export_markdown(params)
  1430	end
  1431	
  1432	--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1433	-- Chat logic
  1434	--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1435	
  1436	---@param buf number | nil # buffer number
  1437	M.prep_md = function(buf)
  1438		-- disable swapping for this buffer and set filetype to markdown
  1439		vim.api.nvim_command("setlocal noswapfile")
  1440		-- better text wrapping
  1441		vim.api.nvim_command("setlocal wrap linebreak")
  1442		if type(M.config.repo_root) == "string" and M.config.repo_root ~= "" then
  1443			require("parley.neighborhood").attach_completion(buf)
  1444		end
  1445		-- auto save on TextChanged, InsertLeave (debounced to avoid disk thrashing on large files)
  1446		local save_timer = nil
  1447		local SAVE_DEBOUNCE_MS = 1000
  1448		vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd({ "TextChanged", "InsertLeave" }, {
  1449			buffer = buf,
  1450			callback = function()
  1451				if save_timer then
  1452					stop_and_close_timer(save_timer)
  1453				end
  1454				local timer = vim.uv.new_timer()
  1455				save_timer = timer
  1456				timer:start(
  1457					SAVE_DEBOUNCE_MS,
  1458					0,
  1459					vim.schedule_wrap(function()
  1460						stop_and_close_timer(timer)
  1925		end
  1926	
  1927		M.refresh_state({ last_chat = file_name })
  1928		if M._prepared_bufs[buf] then
  1929			-- 	M.logger.debug("buffer already prepared: " .. buf)
  1930			return
  1931		end
  1932		M._prepared_bufs[buf] = true
  1933	
  1934		M.prep_md(buf)
  1935	
  1936		-- Spellcheck + as-you-type spell-suggestion typeahead (config.chat_spell).
  1937		-- The spell <CR> map shadows interview's global <CR> map buffer-locally, so we
  1938		-- inject interview.cr_keys as the no-popup base to keep timestamp insertion (#134).
  1939		local cs = M.config.chat_spell
  1940		if cs and (cs.enable or cs.typeahead) then
  1941			require("parley.spell").attach(buf, vim.tbl_extend("force", cs, {
  1942				prompt_buf_type = M.config.chat_prompt_buf_type,
  1943				base_cr = require("parley.interview").cr_keys,
  1944			}))
  1945		end
  1946	
  1947		-- Set up tool block folding (clickable foldcolumn icons)
  1948		require("parley.tool_folds").setup(buf)
  1949	
  1950		require("parley.neighborhood").attach_completion(buf)
  1951	
  1952		if M.config.chat_prompt_buf_type then
  1953			vim.api.nvim_set_option_value("buftype", "prompt", { buf = buf })
  1954			vim.fn.prompt_setprompt(buf, "")
  1955			vim.fn.prompt_setcallback(buf, function()
  1956				M.cmd.ChatRespond({ args = "" })
  1957			end)
  1958		end
  1959	
  1960		-- Register chat buffer-local keymaps from registry
  1961		-- Helper: make respond callback for a given command name
  1962		local function make_respond_cb(command_name)
  1963			local cmd_str = M.config.cmd_prefix .. command_name
  1964			local range_cmd = ":<C-u>'<,'>" .. cmd_str .. "<cr>"
  1965			return {
  1966				n = function()
  1967					vim.api.nvim_command(cmd_str)
  1968					vim.api.nvim_command("stopinsert")
  1969					M.helpers.feedkeys("<esc>", "xn")
  1970				end,

     1	-- Integration test for lua/parley/skill_invoke.lua — the thin P2 driver.
     2	--
     3	-- Reuses the chat_respond_spec fake pattern: monkeypatch parley.dispatcher.query
     4	-- (the LLM dispatcher), inject a tool-use raw_response into tasker, fire on_exit;
     5	-- vim.wait for the (vim.scheduled) on_done. The propose_edits call applies through
     6	-- the REAL tools dispatcher (execute_call) onto the artifact file.
     7	
     8	local skill_invoke = require("parley.skill_invoke")
     9	local parley = require("parley")
    10	local tasker = require("parley.tasker")
    11	local assembly = require("parley.skill_assembly")
    12	
    13	-- SSE builder (same shape as tests/unit/anthropic_tool_decode_spec.lua).
    14	local function sse(events)
    15	    local out = {}
    16	    for _, ev in ipairs(events) do
    17	        table.insert(out, "event: " .. (ev.type or "unknown"))
    18	        table.insert(out, "data: " .. vim.json.encode(ev))
    19	        table.insert(out, "")
    20	    end
    21	    return table.concat(out, "\n")
    22	end
    23	
    24	-- A raw_response decoding to one propose_edits tool call with the given edits.
    25	local function propose_edits_sse(edits)
    26	    return sse({
    27	        { type = "content_block_start", index = 0,
    28	          content_block = { type = "tool_use", id = "t1", name = "propose_edits", input = {} } },
    29	        { type = "content_block_delta", index = 0,
    30	          delta = { type = "input_json_delta", partial_json = vim.json.encode({ edits = edits }) } },
    31	        { type = "content_block_stop", index = 0 },
    32	        { type = "message_stop" },
    33	    })
    34	end
    35	
    36	local function read_file_sse(path)
    37	    return sse({
    38	        { type = "content_block_start", index = 0,
    39	          content_block = { type = "tool_use", id = "t_read", name = "read_file", input = {} } },
    40	        { type = "content_block_delta", index = 0,
    41	          delta = { type = "input_json_delta", partial_json = vim.json.encode({ file_path = path }) } },
    42	        { type = "content_block_stop", index = 0 },
    43	        { type = "message_stop" },
    44	    })
    45	end
    46	
    47	local function manifest(over)
    48	    return vim.tbl_extend("force", {
    49	        name = "t", description = "d", scope = "global", activation = { manual = true },
    50	        source = function() return "SYSTEM BODY" end,
    51	        tools = {}, elevated = { "propose_edits" }, force_tool = "propose_edits",
    52	    }, over or {})
    53	end
    54	
    55	describe("skill_invoke.invoke", function()
    56	    local tmpdir, path, buf
    57	    local orig_query, orig_resolve
    58	    local captured_payload, done_result
    59	
    60	    before_each(function()
    61	        require("parley.tools").register_builtins() -- propose_edits must be registered
    62	        tmpdir = vim.fn.tempname() .. "-si"
    63	        vim.fn.mkdir(tmpdir, "p")
    64	        path = tmpdir .. "/doc.md"
    65	        vim.fn.writefile({ "alpha beta" }, path)
    66	        vim.cmd("edit " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(path))
    67	        buf = vim.api.nvim_get_current_buf()
    68	
    69	        captured_payload, done_result = nil, nil
    70	
    71	        -- isolate from real agent resolution (tested separately in M2)
    72	        orig_resolve = assembly.resolve_agent
    73	        assembly.resolve_agent = function()
    74	            return { model = "m", provider = "anthropic" }
    75	        end
    76	
    77	        orig_query = parley.dispatcher.query
    78	        parley.dispatcher.query = function(_buf, _provider, payload, _handler, on_exit)
    79	            captured_payload = payload
    80	            tasker.set_query("qid_test", {
    81	                raw_response = propose_edits_sse({
    82	                    { old_string = "alpha", new_string = "ALPHA", explain = "uppercase" },
    83	                }),
    84	            })
    85	            vim.schedule(function()
    86	                on_exit("qid_test")
    87	            end)
    88	        end
    89	    end)
    90	
    91	    after_each(function()
    92	        parley.dispatcher.query = orig_query
    93	        assembly.resolve_agent = orig_resolve
    94	        pcall(function() require("parley.progress").stop() end)
    95	        vim.fn.delete(tmpdir, "rf")
    96	    end)
    97	
    98	    it("drives one exchange: payload + force_tool, applies propose_edits, reloads, on_done", function()
    99	        skill_invoke.invoke(buf, manifest(), {}, {
   100	            manual = true,
   101	            on_done = function(r) done_result = r end,
   102	        })
   103	        vim.wait(2000, function() return done_result ~= nil end)
   104	
   105	        assert.is_not_nil(done_result, "on_done never ran")
   106	        assert.is_true(done_result.ok)
   107	        -- payload: force_tool → tool_choice; the system body is messages[1]
   108	        assert.are.same({ type = "tool", name = "propose_edits" }, captured_payload.tool_choice)
   109	        -- large-document headroom: max_tokens bumped well past the 4096 default
   110	        assert.is_true((captured_payload.max_tokens or 0) >= 100000)
   111	        -- propose_edits applied to the artifact FILE via the real execute_call path
   112	        assert.are.equal("ALPHA beta", table.concat(vim.fn.readfile(path), "\n"))
   113	        -- the artifact BUFFER was reloaded to match
   114	        assert.are.equal("ALPHA beta", table.concat(vim.api.nvim_buf_get_lines(buf, 0, -1, false), "\n"))
   115	        -- a numbered backup was made (M3 Task 1)
   116	        assert.are.equal(1, vim.fn.filereadable(path .. ".parley-backup.1"))
   117	        -- #133 M3: on_done payload carries the journal-feeding fields
   118	        assert.are.equal("alpha beta", done_result.original)
   119	        assert.are.equal("ALPHA beta", done_result.new_content)
   120	        assert.is_true(#done_result.decorations >= 1)
   121	        assert.are.equal("edit", done_result.decorations[1].kind)
   122	        assert.are.equal("uppercase", done_result.decorations[1].explain)
   123	    end)
   124	
   125	    it("coerces a stringified edits array and applies it (model quirk, #133)", function()
   126	        -- Some models emit the propose_edits `edits` arg as a JSON STRING, not an
   127	        -- array. The driver must coerce it (so it applies) and not crash the renderer.
   128	        parley.dispatcher.query = function(_b, _p, _payload, _h, on_exit)
   129	            local edits_str = vim.json.encode({ { old_string = "alpha", new_string = "ALPHA", explain = "up" } })
   130	            tasker.set_query("qid_str", {
   131	                raw_response = sse({
   132	                    { type = "content_block_start", index = 0,
   133	                      content_block = { type = "tool_use", id = "t1", name = "propose_edits", input = {} } },
   134	                    { type = "content_block_delta", index = 0,
   135	                      delta = { type = "input_json_delta", partial_json = vim.json.encode({ edits = edits_str }) } },
   136	                    { type = "content_block_stop", index = 0 },
   137	                    { type = "message_stop" },
   138	                }),
   139	            })
   140	            vim.schedule(function() on_exit("qid_str") end)
   141	        end
   142	        skill_invoke.invoke(buf, manifest(), {}, { on_done = function(r) done_result = r end })
   143	        vim.wait(2000, function() return done_result ~= nil end)
   144	        assert.is_not_nil(done_result, "on_done never ran (renderer likely crashed)")
   145	        assert.is_true(done_result.ok)
   146	        assert.are.equal("ALPHA beta", table.concat(vim.fn.readfile(path), "\n"))
   147	    end)
   148	
   149	    it("surfaces a failed edit: on_done ok=false, applied=0, file untouched", function()
   150	        -- non-unique old_string → compute_edits fails → propose_edits is_error
   151	        vim.fn.writefile({ "ab ab" }, path)
   152	        vim.cmd("edit! " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(path))
   153	        buf = vim.api.nvim_get_current_buf()
   154	        parley.dispatcher.query = function(_b, _p, _payload, _h, on_exit)
   155	            tasker.set_query("qid_err", {
   156	                raw_response = propose_edits_sse({
   157	                    { old_string = "ab", new_string = "X", explain = "non-unique" },
   158	                }),
   159	            })
   160	            vim.schedule(function() on_exit("qid_err") end)
   161	        end
   162	        skill_invoke.invoke(buf, manifest(), {}, { on_done = function(r) done_result = r end })
   163	        vim.wait(2000, function() return done_result ~= nil end)
   164	        assert.is_not_nil(done_result)
   165	        assert.is_false(done_result.ok)
   166	        assert.are.equal(0, done_result.applied)
   167	        assert.are.equal("ab ab", table.concat(vim.fn.readfile(path), "\n")) -- untouched
   168	    end)
   169	
   170	    it("is_in_flight true during a query; cancel clears it + supersedes the exchange (#133)", function()
   171	        local held_exit
   172	        parley.dispatcher.query = function(_b, _p, _payload, _h, on_exit)
   173	            held_exit = on_exit -- hold it open; don't complete the query
   174	        end
   175	        skill_invoke.invoke(buf, manifest(), {}, { on_done = function(r) done_result = r end })
   176	        assert.is_true(skill_invoke.is_in_flight(buf))
   177	        skill_invoke.cancel(buf)
   178	        assert.is_false(skill_invoke.is_in_flight(buf))
   179	        -- The now-superseded query completes late → its on_exit must no-op.
   180	        tasker.set_query("qid_stale", { raw_response = "" })
   180	        assert.matches("line 2", text)
   181	    end)
   182	
   183	    it("increments iter counter on recurse", function()
   184	        local bufnr = mk_buffer({ "💬: q", "🤖: [Claude]" })
   185	        local raw = mk_read_file_sse_response("toolu_ITER", scratch_file)
   186	        tool_loop.process_response(bufnr, raw, { max_tool_iterations = 20, cwd = tmp_base })
   187	        assert.equals(1, tool_loop.get_iter(bufnr))
   188	    end)
   189	
   190	    it("stops with 'done' when max_tool_iterations is hit (cap behavior)", function()
   191	        local bufnr = mk_buffer({ "💬: q", "🤖: [Claude]" })
   192	        -- Pre-set iter to the cap
   193	        tool_loop.increment_iter(bufnr)
   194	        tool_loop.increment_iter(bufnr)
   195	        tool_loop.increment_iter(bufnr)
   196	        assert.equals(3, tool_loop.get_iter(bufnr))
   197	
   198	        local raw = mk_read_file_sse_response("toolu_CAP", scratch_file)
   199	        local outcome = tool_loop.process_response(bufnr, raw, { max_tool_iterations = 3, cwd = tmp_base })
   200	        -- At the cap, process_response returns "done" without recursing further.
   201	        -- (M4 Task 4.1 will add a synthetic 📎: (iteration limit reached)
   202	        -- result for better UX; M2 just stops.)
   203	        assert.equals("done", outcome)
   204	    end)
   205	
   206	    it("handles tool execution errors gracefully (cwd-scope rejection)", function()
   207	        -- A path OUTSIDE cwd should trigger dispatcher.execute_call's
   208	        -- safety prelude which returns an error ToolResult. tool_loop
   209	        -- still writes the 📎: error block and continues.
   210	        require("parley.config").tool_read_roots = {}
   211	        local bufnr = mk_buffer({ "💬: q", "🤖: [Claude]" })
   212	        -- Path that's not inside cwd — construct a sibling tmp file
   213	        local outside = tmp_base .. "/outside-" .. math.random(0, 0xFFFFFF) .. ".txt"
   214	        vim.fn.writefile({ "secret" }, outside)
   215	        -- Set cwd to a narrow dir that doesn't contain `outside`
   216	        local cwd = tmp_base .. "/narrow-" .. math.random(0, 0xFFFFFF)
   217	        vim.fn.mkdir(cwd, "p")
   218	
   219	        local raw = mk_read_file_sse_response("toolu_ERR", outside)
   220	        local outcome = tool_loop.process_response(bufnr, raw, {
   221	            max_tool_iterations = 20,
   222	            cwd = cwd,
   223	        })
   224	        assert.equals("recurse", outcome)
   225	
   226	        local text = buf_text(bufnr)
   227	        assert.matches("🔧: read_file id=toolu_ERR", text)
   228	        assert.matches("📎: read_file id=toolu_ERR", text)
   229	        assert.matches("error=true", text)
   230	        assert.matches("read path .* configured roots", text)
   231	    end)
   232	
   233	    it("uses the chat buffer neighborhood as cwd when agent cwd is absent", function()
   234	        local parley = require("parley")
   235	        local repo = tmp_base .. "/repo-" .. math.random(0, 0xFFFFFF)
   236	        local repo_chat = repo .. "/workshop/parley"
   237	        local other_cwd = tmp_base .. "/other-cwd-" .. math.random(0, 0xFFFFFF)
   238	        vim.fn.mkdir(repo_chat, "p")
   239	        vim.fn.mkdir(other_cwd, "p")
   240	        vim.fn.writefile({ "from repo root" }, repo .. "/README.md")
   241	
   242	        parley.config.repo_root = repo
   243	        parley.config.repo_chat_dir = "workshop/parley"
   244	
   245	        local bufnr = mk_buffer({ "💬: q", "🤖: [Claude]" })
   246	        vim.api.nvim_buf_set_name(bufnr, repo_chat .. "/2026-06-29.topic.md")
   247	
   248	        local old_cwd = vim.fn.getcwd()
   249	        vim.cmd("cd " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(other_cwd))
   250	        local raw = mk_read_file_sse_response("toolu_NEIGHBORHOOD", "README.md")
   251	        local outcome = tool_loop.process_response(bufnr, raw, {
   252	            max_tool_iterations = 20,
   253	        })
   254	        vim.cmd("cd " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(old_cwd))
   255	
   256	        assert.equals("recurse", outcome)
   257	        local text = buf_text(bufnr)
   258	        assert.matches("📎: read_file id=toolu_NEIGHBORHOOD", text)
   259	        assert.matches("from repo root", text)
   260	    end)
   261	
   262	    it("emits 📎: result in dynamic-fence form that survives backticks in file content", function()
   263	        -- Put backticks in the scratch file content to verify the
   264	        -- fence-length logic from serialize.render_result picks a
   265	        -- fence longer than any backtick run in the body.
   266	        local back_file = tmp_base .. "/back-" .. math.random(0, 0xFFFFFF) .. ".md"
   267	        vim.fn.writefile({ "```lua", "local x = 1", "```" }, back_file)
   268	
   269	        local bufnr = mk_buffer({ "💬: q", "🤖: [Claude]" })
   270	        local raw = mk_read_file_sse_response("toolu_BACK", back_file)
   240	git commit -m "#181: resolve reads across ordered roots"
   241	```

   214	    end)

   216	    it("aborts gracefully (on_done ok=false) when source() throws", function()
   217	        -- a fallible source (e.g. voice_apply with a missing style file) must route
   218	        -- through on_done, not throw a raw Lua error past the caller.
   219	        local query_called = false
   220	        parley.dispatcher.query = function() query_called = true end
   221	        local m = manifest({ source = function() error("style file not found") end })
   222	        skill_invoke.invoke(buf, m, {}, { on_done = function(r) done_result = r end })
   223	        vim.wait(500, function() return done_result ~= nil end)
   224	        assert.is_not_nil(done_result, "on_done must run even when source throws")
   225	        assert.is_false(done_result.ok)
   226	        assert.is_truthy((done_result.msg or ""):find("source", 1, true))
   227	        assert.is_false(query_called, "must not query when source failed")
   228	    end)
   229	
   230	    it("executes relative tool paths from a repo-backed artifact neighborhood", function()
   231	        local repo = tmpdir .. "/repo"
   232	        local repo_chat = repo .. "/workshop/parley"
   233	        vim.fn.mkdir(repo_chat, "p")
   234	        vim.fn.writefile({ "repo root file" }, repo .. "/README.md")
   235	        path = repo_chat .. "/2026-06-29.topic.md"
   236	        vim.fn.writefile({ "alpha beta" }, path)
   237	        vim.cmd("edit! " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(path))
   238	        buf = vim.api.nvim_get_current_buf()
   239	
   240	        parley.config.repo_root = repo
   241	        parley.config.repo_chat_dir = "workshop/parley"
   242	
   243	        parley.dispatcher.query = function(_b, _p, _payload, _h, on_exit)
   244	            tasker.set_query("qid_read", {
   245	                raw_response = read_file_sse("README.md"),
   246	            })
   247	            vim.schedule(function() on_exit("qid_read") end)
   248	        end
   249	
   250	        skill_invoke.invoke(buf, manifest({
   251	            tools = { "read_file" },
   252	            elevated = {},
   253	            force_tool = "read_file",
   254	        }), {}, { on_done = function(r) done_result = r end })
   255	        vim.wait(2000, function() return done_result ~= nil end)
   256	
   257	        assert.is_not_nil(done_result)
   258	        assert.is_true(done_result.ok)
   259	        assert.equals("repo root file", done_result.results[1].content:match("repo root file"))
   260	    end)
   261	
   262	    it("executes relative tool paths from a super-repo sibling chat neighborhood", function()
   263	        local current_repo = tmpdir .. "/current"
   264	        local sibling_repo = tmpdir .. "/sibling"
   265	        local current_chat = current_repo .. "/workshop/parley"
   266	        local sibling_chat = sibling_repo .. "/workshop/parley"
   267	        vim.fn.mkdir(current_chat, "p")
   268	        vim.fn.mkdir(sibling_chat, "p")
   269	        vim.fn.writefile({ "sibling repo root file" }, sibling_repo .. "/README.md")
   270	        path = sibling_chat .. "/2026-06-29.topic.md"
   271	        vim.fn.writefile({ "alpha beta" }, path)
   272	        vim.cmd("edit! " .. vim.fn.fnameescape(path))
   273	        buf = vim.api.nvim_get_current_buf()
   274	
   275	        parley.config.repo_root = current_repo
   276	        parley.config.repo_chat_dir = "workshop/parley"
   277	        parley.config.chat_roots = {
   278	            { dir = current_chat, label = "repo" },
   279	            { dir = sibling_chat, label = "sibling" },
   280	        }
   281	
   282	        parley.dispatcher.query = function(_b, _p, _payload, _h, on_exit)
   283	            tasker.set_query("qid_sibling_read", {
   284	                raw_response = read_file_sse("README.md"),
   285	            })
   286	            vim.schedule(function() on_exit("qid_sibling_read") end)
   287	        end
   288	
   289	        skill_invoke.invoke(buf, manifest({
   290	            tools = { "read_file" },
   291	            elevated = {},
   292	            force_tool = "read_file",
   293	        }), {}, { on_done = function(r) done_result = r end })
   294	        vim.wait(2000, function() return done_result ~= nil end)
   295	
   296	        assert.is_not_nil(done_result)
   297	        assert.is_true(done_result.ok)
   298	        assert.equals("sibling repo root file", done_result.results[1].content:match("sibling repo root file"))
   299	    end)
   300	end)

The implementation appears correct and cohesive: one root policy now drives read resolution, write confinement, completion, skill/chat dispatch, and model guidance. Atlas coverage is present, the focused feature tests pass within the full run, and no Critical correctness defect was found. Before closing, cheaply strengthen the integration evidence for the issue’s central behavior: current chat and skill wiring tests use repo-backed chat artifacts whose neighborhood is already the repo root, so they do not prove widening from an ordinary nested Markdown neighborhood.

1. Strengths

- `resolve_read_path` correctly enforces ordered, first-existing resolution and rejects escaping or unresolved symlinks ([dispatcher.lua:127](/Users/xianxu/workspace/worktree/parley.nvim/000181-repo-root-read-wide-completion-for-all-markdown/lua/parley/tools/dispatcher.lua:127)).
- Read and write handling remain explicitly separated at the shared dispatcher boundary ([dispatcher.lua:286](/Users/xianxu/workspace/worktree/parley.nvim/000181-repo-root-read-wide-completion-for-all-markdown/lua/parley/tools/dispatcher.lua:286)).
- Completion uses the same resolver as tool enforcement, preserving the “suggested paths are accepted paths” invariant ([neighborhood.lua:202](/Users/xianxu/workspace/worktree/parley.nvim/000181-repo-root-read-wide-completion-for-all-markdown/lua/parley/neighborhood.lua:202)).
- Attachment is buffer-idempotent and ordinary non-repo Markdown remains untouched ([neighborhood.lua:308](/Users/xianxu/workspace/worktree/parley.nvim/000181-repo-root-read-wide-completion-for-all-markdown/lua/parley/neighborhood.lua:308), [init.lua:1437](/Users/xianxu/workspace/worktree/parley.nvim/000181-repo-root-read-wide-completion-for-all-markdown/lua/parley/init.lua:1437)).
- Both required atlas surfaces and traceability were updated. No README update is required because no new command, keybinding, flag, or configuration key was introduced.

2. Critical findings

None.

3. Important findings

- Missing central wiring regression: the plan requires chat and skill tests from a non-artifact Markdown buffer nested below the repo root ([plan.md:254](/Users/xianxu/workspace/worktree/parley.nvim/000181-repo-root-read-wide-completion-for-all-markdown/workshop/plans/000181-repo-root-read-wide-completion-for-all-markdown-plan.md:254)), but the landed chat test uses `workshop/parley`, whose existing neighborhood already resolves to the repo root ([tool_loop_spec.lua:233](/Users/xianxu/workspace/worktree/parley.nvim/000181-repo-root-read-wide-completion-for-all-markdown/tests/unit/tool_loop_spec.lua:233)), and the skill test does likewise ([skill_invoke_spec.lua:230](/Users/xianxu/workspace/worktree/parley.nvim/000181-repo-root-read-wide-completion-for-all-markdown/tests/integration/skill_invoke_spec.lua:230)). These tests would pass under the old single-root behavior and therefore do not catch a wiring regression that drops `repo_root` for ordinary `data/.../*.md` buffers. Add at least one chat/tool-loop and one skill invocation from `repo/data/nested/doc.md`, reading a path present only at `repo/README.md` or `repo/atlas/...`; also assert a write to that same relative path remains rejected. This is an `ARCH-PURPOSE` evidence gap, not an implementation defect.

4. Minor findings

- Dispatcher comments and annotations still describe the older `cwd`/`allowed_roots` API and “M2” state around [dispatcher.lua:9](/Users/xianxu/workspace/worktree/parley.nvim/000181-repo-root-read-wide-completion-for-all-markdown/lua/parley/tools/dispatcher.lua:9) and [dispatcher.lua:241](/Users/xianxu/workspace/worktree/parley.nvim/000181-repo-root-read-wide-completion-for-all-markdown/lua/parley/tools/dispatcher.lua:241). Refresh them when convenient.

5. Test coverage notes

`make -f Makefile.parley test` showed all feature-related unit and integration specs passing, with lint reporting 0 warnings/errors across 244 files. The overall command was not green because unrelated cliproxy end-to-end tests attempted to connect to `127.0.0.1:0`; this looks environmental and outside this diff, but the close record should only claim a full green run after the harness dependency is available or the established test configuration is used.

The resolver tests themselves cover collision precedence, missing reads, absolute paths, escaping and dangling symlinks, policy-only dispatch, scalar fields, arrays, defaults, tool kind, and narrow writes.

6. Architectural notes for upcoming work

- `ARCH-DRY`: Pass. Root derivation, model guidance, completion, and dispatcher enforcement share `RootPolicy`; completion acceptance reuses `resolve_read_path`.
- `ARCH-PURE`: Pass. `build_policy`, `merge_completion_candidates`, and `format_tool_context` are directly tested deterministic helpers. Filesystem probing and Neovim/cmp attachment remain integration code. The Core Concepts PURE/INTEGRATION classifications match the implementation.
- `ARCH-PURPOSE`: Implementation passes the shadow sweep: chat, recursive responses, skills, built-in completion, cmp completion, and guidance derive from the shared policy. Test evidence needs the ordinary nested-Markdown cases noted above before the boundary record fully demonstrates the issue’s purpose.

7. Plan revision recommendations

Add a `## Revisions` entry such as:

> **Close review — exercise actual widening at integration seams:** Existing chat and skill tests used repo-backed artifacts whose legacy neighborhood was already the repo root, so they did not distinguish the new policy from old behavior. Added nested ordinary-Markdown chat/skill cases proving repo-root reads succeed while writes remain neighborhood-confined (`ARCH-PURPOSE`).

Also mark the relevant durable-plan checklist steps complete once those tests and the final verification have actually run.
